threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always tell a newbie by how they still ask which bus to take during rush hour instead of just hopping on the first one that comes.
Last week, overheard a guy on the N47 confessing his 'foul-smelling' Chinese takeout was how he treats a breakup. Night buses really do attract the best stories.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a cat sitting on a bicycle in front of the Späti, patiently waiting for its owner. That’s when it clicked—I'm home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find the spontaneous street parties in summer, complete with DJs on rooftops and late-night currywurst stands popping up out of nowhere.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Anyone who knows how to dodge the Muni delays while still hitting Dolores for a sunny day knows the city in their bones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a couple on the N-Judah at Stanyan, guitar and all. Only in the Haight would that go from weird to normal in seconds.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the plaça del Sol is just a chill spot. Locals know it's a battleground for finding an empty bench on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they stare wide-eyed at the metro maps, still trying to figure out which line is which.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the B&Bs are a holiday option, not a year-long trap. The council’s just given out payouts; welcome to reality.
Saw a guy on the Night Bus 77 serenading passengers with a ukulele. Best part was the random harmonies from a couple of tipsy friends. Classic Brixton vibe!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a broken rollator as a makeshift bike ramp on the street—only in Neukölln does play have such creativity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can get a döner kebab at 4 AM after a night out, but only in Neukölln. It's like a rite of passage here—life's best moments often happen when you're half-asleep.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the Muni schedule – they know the real trick is avoiding the bus at all costs during the noon rush.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy serenading a Muni bus stop with a ukulele. He closed with a bow, and the bus arrived right on cue. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that siesta isn’t just for shops; some locals actually take a nap in the park, especially by the pond. Pure bliss.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always clutch a guidebook tighter than a local would hold a pint, still figuring out when to say 'gràcies' without sounding too formal.
